mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 signaling (including FLT3-ITD-driven proliferation)
additional_notes: Approved in AML combination therapy; highlighted for FLT3-ITD dependence in MOLM-13

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 to suppress growth of FLT3-ITD AML cells
additional_notes: Approved in AML; noted as strong in FLT3-ITD AML lines

mechanism: Potent inhibition of FLT3 (including FLT3-ITD) leading to reduced AML cell proliferation
additional_notes: Approved in some regions; described as highly potent vs FLT3-ITD models

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases; can inhibit FLT3 signaling in FLT3-ITD AML contexts
additional_notes: Current use: HCC/RCC; mentioned with off-label/repurpose history in FLT3-ITD AML

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3; suppresses signaling in FLT3-driven AML models
additional_notes: Listed as BCR-ABL; multi-kinase including FLT3

mechanism: Inhibits SRC/ABL-family kinases; can suppress signaling nodes relevant in subsets of AML
Inhibits MEK, blocking MAPK signaling to reduce proliferation
Inhibits BCL-2 to promote apoptosis in BCL-2–dependent cells
additional_notes: Approved in AML combo; dependence noted as context-dependent in MOLM-13

mechanism: Inhibits BCL-2 and BCL-xL to induce apoptosis
additional_notes: Alternative name: ABT-263; described as broader BH3 mimetic

mechanism: Inhibits MDM2, activating p53 pathway in TP53-wildtype settings
additional_notes: Clinical-stage; note to verify TP53 status (p53-WT AML)

mechanism: Blocks MDM2–p53 interaction to activate p53 signaling
additional_notes: Described as tool/clinical precursor; common positive control for MDM2–p53 axis

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases causing epigenetic modulation and growth arrest/apoptosis
additional_notes: Current use noted as myeloma; cited for epigenetic vulnerability in AML

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases leading to epigenetic changes affecting proliferation
additional_notes: Alternative name: SAHA; current use CTCL; noted variable single-agent AML activity

category: DNA hypomethylating agent (DNMT inhibitor; nucleoside analog)
mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and inhibits DNA methylation, leading to epigenetic reprogramming and antiproliferative effects
additional_notes: Time-dependent; note: often needs 72–120 h exposure

mechanism: Hypomethylating nucleoside analog causing epigenetic modulation and reduced proliferation with delayed onset
additional_notes: Noted slower onset; often needs longer exposure

mechanism: Inhibits the proteasome, inducing proteotoxic stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Current use: myeloma; also suggested as a positive control option

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome, inducing proteostasis stress and cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Described as potent with short half-life but strong in vitro activity

mechanism: Inhibits JAK/STAT pathway signaling contributing to AML survival in some settings
Inhibits JAK1/2 to reduce JAK/STAT signaling and proliferation in susceptible AML subsets
additional_notes: Current use: MPN; activity noted as subset-dependent

mechanism: Modulates mitochondrial metabolism/AMPK-related pathways; antiproliferative effects at higher in vitro doses
additional_notes: Current use: type 2 diabetes; note that millimolar ranges are typically needed in standard glucose media; consider lower-glucose/OXPHOS-reliant conditions
